channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Bitcoin ticks closer to $100,000 in extended surge following US elections
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Bitcoin extended its streak of record highs after ticking above $99,000 for the first time. The cryptocurrency has rocketed more than 40% in just two weeks.
item.dc:creator: WYATTE GRANTHAM-PHILIPS, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Risk and Compliance Exchange 2024: DoD’ Stacy Bostjanick, DCMA’s Jennifer Henderson on finding ‘any means possible’ to help small biz with CMMC
item.description: As the Cybersecurity Maturity Model Certification program inches closer to reality, DoD prepares defense industrial base for compliance requirements.
item.dc:creator: Justin Doubleday, Federal News Network

item.title: JDK 24: The new features in Java 24
item.description: In the wake of Java Development Kit (JDK) 23, which arrived September 17, work now focuses on the planned successor release, JDK 24, which has ballooned to 24 proposed features. The four most recent additions include warnings upon the use of memory-access methods in sun.misc.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Archaeologists discover 4,000-year-old canals used to fish by predecessors of ancient Maya
item.description: Using drones and Google Earth imagery, archaeologists have discovered a 4,000-year-old network of earthen canals in what’s now Belize.
item.dc:creator: Christina Larson, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: The future according to Google DeepMind CEO Demis Hassabis
item.description: Google DeepMind CEO Demis Hassabis used to get some of his most fulfilling work done between midnight and 3 a.m. A self-described “nocturnal person,” the Londoner devoted the solitude of his overnight shift to catching up on scientific papers, coming up with new ideas, and just plain thinking.
item.dc:creator: Harry McCracken, Fast Company

item.title: Apple readies more conversational Siri in bid to catch up in AI
item.description: Apple has been testing the upgraded software on iPhones, iPads and Macs as a separate app, but the technology will ultimately replace the Siri interface that users rely on today.
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg, The Mercury News

item.title: AI agents are unlike any technology ever
item.description: The agents are coming, and they represent a fundamental shift in the role artificial intelligence plays in businesses, governments, and our lives. The biggest news in agentic AI happened this month when we learned that OpenAI’s agent, Operator, is expected to launch in January.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Data Architects: Key Responsibilities, Skills, and Salary Ranges
item.description: Discover the role of data architects in organizations, their key responsibilities, skills required, salary ranges, and certifications.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Australia rejects Elon Musk's claim that it plans to control access to the internet
item.description: An Australian Cabinet minister has rejected X Corp. owner Elon Musk’s allegation that the government intends to control all Australians' access to the internet through legislation that would ban young children from social media.
item.dc:creator: Rod Mcguirk, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Gary Gensler said he’ll step down as SEC chair. Bitcoin is soaring
item.description: Crypto enthusiasts are ecstatic after U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) Chair Gary Gensler said he will step down from his post on January 20 once President-elect Donald Trump takes office.
item.dc:creator: Jessica Bursztynsky, Fast Company

item.title: AI networking a focus of HPE’s Juniper deal as Justice Department concerns swirl
item.description: Amid reports of US regulatory concerns over HPE’s proposed buy of Juniper Networks, the CEOs of both companies talked about the future of the blended entity at this week’s HPE Discover event in Barcelona – and that future includes plenty of AI networking technology.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Advanced programming with Java generics
item.description: Generics in Java enhance the type safety of your code and make it easier to read. In my last article, I introduced the general concepts of generics and showed examples from the Java Collections Framework. You also learned how to use generics to avoid runtime errors like the ClassCastException.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: US gathers allies to talk AI safety as Trump's vow to undo Biden's AI policy overshadows their work
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (AP) — President-elect Donald Trump has vowed to repeal President Joe Biden's signature artificial intelligence policy when he returns to the White House for a second term. What that actually means for the future of AI technology remains to be seen.
item.dc:creator: MATT O'BRIEN and BARBARA ORTUTAY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Meta's Threads app launches custom feeds amid Bluesky surge
item.description: The rollout came just days after Meta CEO Mark Zuckerberg announced last week that Threads was testing the new feature.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, NewsNation

item.title: Australia won't force social media users to share their personal details when child ban takes effect
item.description: MELBOURNE, Australia (AP) — Australia's communications minister said Wednesday the government won't force social media users to hand over their personal information to tech companies, as children younger than 16 are set to be banned from the platforms.
item.dc:creator: ROD McGUIRK, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: An Android-ChromeOS merger might actually make sense now
item.description: Stop me if you’ve heard this one: Google’s got two primary user-facing platforms, Android and ChromeOS — and, if the latest rumors are to be believed, the company may be on the brink of bringing ’em together and combining ’em into a single, streamlined entity.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Microsoft unveils imaging APIs for Windows Copilot Runtime
item.description: Microsoft’s Windows Copilot Runtime, which allows developers to integrate AI capabilities into Windows, is being fitted with AI-backed APIs for image processing. It will also gain access to Phi 3.5 Silica, a custom-built generative AI model for Copilot+ PCs.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: White House Initiative Enters New Phase, Government Efficiency Plans Unveiled
item.description: The White House-led initiative to overhaul the security clearance and personnel vetting process has entered a new phase, while plans for a Department of Government Efficiency are being drawn up.
item.dc:creator: Michele Sandiford, Federal News Network

item.title: Data breach exposes over 56 million clothing store customers
item.description: Breach site confirmed 56,904,909 Hot Topic users' data leaked online. Tech expert Kurt “CyberGuy" Knutsson says the company's silence makes matters even worse.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Microsoft rebrands Azure AI Studio to Azure AI Foundry
item.description: Microsoft is packaging its Azure AI Studio and other updates into a new service — Azure AI Foundry in response to enterprises’ need to develop, run, and manage generative AI applications.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: German police break up an environmental activists' camp near Tesla plant outside Berlin
item.description: BERLIN (AP) — German police broke up a protest camp housing environmental activists in a forest near the Tesla electric car factory in Grünheide outside Berlin on Tuesday, citing violations of public safety and order.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: F# 9 adds nullable reference types
item.description: F# 9, the latest version of Microsoft’s open source functional language, has been released with support for nullable reference types and empty-bodied computation expressions. Standard library improvements also are featured, including random sampling functions.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Company affiliated with Alex Jones seeks to disqualify The Onion's auction bid on Infowars
item.description: A company affiliated with conspiracy theorist Alex Jones asked a federal judge on Monday to disqualify a bid by the satirical news outlet The Onion to buy Jones' Infowars at a bankruptcy auction, alleging fraud and collusion.
item.dc:creator: DAVE COLLINS, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Judge tosses Huckabee lawsuit against Meta over ads suggesting he endorsed marijuana gummies
item.description: WILMINGTON, Del. (AP) — A federal judge in Delaware on Monday dismissed a lawsuit filed by former Arkansas Gov. Mike Huckabee against social media giant Meta over advertisements using his name and image to sell CBD products.
item.dc:creator: RANDALL CHASE, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: The worst passwords you could have in 2024 — or any year
item.description: Don't rely on easy-to-remember passwords too much — because all of us tend to think more alike than we'd think.
item.dc:creator: Russell Falcon, NewsNation
